results.title: ml-intern: Hugging Face's AI agent that automates post-training | Product Hunt
results.content:
Report

Pranav

Hey everyone!   
So I was basically building a fruad detection model using isolation forests. The part that took me hours was feature engineering iterations and retraining after adding new behavioral signals.   
  
I would love to have an agent that reads the relevant papers, fixes the dataset issues, and returns training jobs without literally babysitting.   
Though I had some questions like how does it handle domain specific tabular fraud data vs the benchmark tasks it's been tested on?

Report

Vedang Mirgal

Wow, this looks really cool. Watching an agent go through papers, citations, datasets, experiments, failures, retraining loops, etc. so autonomously genuinely feels like a very different direction for ML workflows. [...] Subscribe

ml-intern

## Hugging Face's AI agent that automates post-training

70 followers

## Hugging Face's AI agent that automates post-training

70 followers

Visit website

LLM Fine Tuning

•

AI Engineer

An open-source AI agent that fully automates post-training: reads arXiv papers, fixes & creates datasets, runs training jobs, debugs failures, and iterates all by itself. Results: +22 pts on GPQA in 10h and +60% on HealthBench. The future of ML research is here.

Promoted

Aymeric Roucher

Folio

Folio [...] I was especially curious about the research side of it though — since a lot of fast-moving ML literature appears first through preprints and experimental results, how does the system avoid overcommitting compute/resources toward ideas that may later turn out to be weak, noisy, or hard to reproduce?

Report

MEENAKSHI DHAVALESWARAPU

What I found interesting here is that it doesn’t stop at generating training code and calling it done. The whole loop of reading papers, fixing datasets, retrying failed runs, and iterating again feels much closer to an actual ML workflow. Also curious how it decides when a problem is coming from low-quality data versus the model or training setup itself.

Report

Abijah Kajabika

results.title: BilberryDB: A No-Code Multimodal Vector Database | Product Hunt
results.content:
Joseph Abraham

Big congrats @bilberrydb on shipping BilberryDB! 🚀 The team's mission to democratize AI building really resonates, especially with your few-shot learning approach that eliminates the need for large datasets. Being able to build useful AI solutions with just a few data samples is a huge breakthrough. The no-code multimodal vector database that handles 3D, video, audio, tabular, and text in one unified space is exactly what the no-code AI community needs. Looking forward to seeing your impact on making AI accessible to everyone. All the best for launch day!

Bilberrydb
BilberryDB

BilberryDB
